resources.string: The app hit an unexpected error while starting up. Details are below.

Understory OTP self-generates a 256-bit master key, self-encrypts it under a hardware-backed Keystore key, and self-binds it to this device\'s screen lock. The master is never displayed and never typed. To unlock the vault, you authenticate with your device biometric or PIN.

Understory OTP keeps your seeds encrypted on this device only. To survive a lost or reset phone — or a biometric / screen-lock change, which by design destroys the key — export a backup after setup: an Understory encrypted file, or an Aegis Authenticator-compatible JSON. There\'s an Export button on the main screen.
Authenticate with your device to bind the vault master key.
Authenticate with your device biometric or PIN.
Device screen lock required.\n\nUnderstory OTP binds the vault key to your device\'s PIN / pattern / biometric. Set up a screen lock in system Settings, then come back.
BiometricPrompt unavailable (status %1$d). Configure a strong biometric or device credential in system Settings.
%1$d third-party accessibility service(s) active. TOTP codes are visible on screen — accessibility services can read them. Tap \"Review\" to open system settings.
Make a backup so a biometric change or a lost phone doesn\'t lose your codes. Tap Export.
Add your first account: tap the + button to scan a QR code from your gallery or paste an otpauth:// link.
Tap a code to copy it. It never appears on screen.
Codes are generated on this device. Nothing syncs to a server.
Save an encrypted copy so a lost phone or a biometric change doesn\'t lose your codes.
Import an Understory, Aegis, Proton, or Google Authenticator export.
Type codes straight into apps without the clipboard.
Seeds are encrypted on this device and unlocked with your device biometric or PIN. Codes are never shown to a server and never rendered as text on screen.
Engineering build only — live event log for troubleshooting.
Code copied — clipboard auto-clear attempted after %1$ds (not guaranteed if the app is killed first)
Code copied — counter advanced to %1$d. Clipboard auto-clear attempted after 60s (not guaranteed if the app is killed first)
Generate next HOTP code and copy it
%1$s %2$s, TOTP, double-tap to copy the current code, long-press to delete
%1$s %2$s, HOTP, use the Generate next code button, long-press to delete
Removing this entry permanently destroys the secret in this vault. You won\'t be able to generate codes for this account again unless you re-enroll with the original issuer or restore from a backup.

Three options:
 • Pick a screenshot of a QR code from your gallery
 • Paste a full otpauth:// URI directly
 • Paste a base32 secret (Google Authenticator-style)

Issuer / account are optional if the QR or URI provides them.
Couldn\'t read a QR from that image. Try a clearer screenshot.
QR decoded — secret populated. Review issuer/account and Save.
Google Auth export had no entries.
Imported %1$d entries (skipped %2$d duplicates) from Google Authenticator%3$s
(batch %1$d/%2$d — re-run import for the remaining batches)
No entries found in that file%1$s.
